info.title: FeatureScript Standard Library - Modeling
info.description: Core modeling types and operations — Context, Id, Query, operations (op*), evaluations (ev*), sketch, primitives

geometry.fs.name: geometry
geometry.fs.description:
This module makes all Onshape Standard Library features and functions available.New Feature Studios begin with an import of this module, 
```
import(path : "onshape/std/geometry.fs", version : "");
```
with current version inserted (e.g. `300.0`). This gives that Feature Studio access to all functions, types, enums, and constants defined in the Onshape Standard Library.

common.fs.name: common
common.fs.description:
This module makes most common Onshape Standard Library functions available. It can be imported in place of `geometry.fs`, 
```
import(path : "onshape/std/common.fs", version : "");
```
into Feature Studios that do not require the full set of Onshape Standard Library features.

symbols.name: Context
symbols.description:
A `Context` is a `builtin` that stores modeling data, including bodies (solids, sheets, wires, and points), their constituent topological entities (faces, edges, and vertices), variables, feature error states, etc.
Every Onshape Part Studio uses a single `Context`. All features, operations, and evaluation functions require a context to operate on. Different contexts do not interact, but data may be transferred from one to another using `opMergeContexts`.
Each context keeps track of the version of the Onshape Standard Library at which it was created. While regenerating a feature that has been "held back" to an older version, the version reported by the context will be the older version, causing subfeatures and operations to emulate old behavior.

symbols.name: canBeContext
symbols.description: Typecheck for `Context`

symbols.name: isAtInitialMixedModelingReleaseVersionOrLater
symbols.description: Returns true if at or after mixed modeling release version.

symbols.name: Id
canBeId
symbols.description: Typecheck for `Id`

symbols.name: newId
symbols.description: Returns an empty id.

symbols.name: makeId
symbols.description: Returns an id specified by the given string.

symbols.name: isTopLevelId
symbols.description: True if the `Id` represents a top-level feature or default geometry (i.e. if the `Id` has length `1`)

symbols.name: ANY_ID
symbols.description:
The string literal `"*"`, which matches any id inside certain queries. 
 Example: 
`qCreatedBy(id + ANY_ID + "fillet")`

symbols.name: unstableIdComponent
symbols.description: Marks a given id component as "unstable" causing queries to treat it as a wildcard. This is useful for when the id component is not expected to be robust, such as an index into the results of an evaluated query.

symbols.name: setVariable
symbols.signature: (context is Context, name is string, value)
symbols.description:
Attach a variable to the context, which can be retrieved by another feature defined later. If a variable of the same name already exists, this function will overwrite it.
 Example: 
`setVariable(context, "foo", 1) attaches a variable named "foo", with value set to 1, on the context.`

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Can be any value, including an array or map with many elements.

symbols.name: setVariable
symbols.signature: (context is Context, name is string, value, description is string)
symbols.description:
Attach a variable with a description to the context, which can be retrieved by another feature defined later. If a variable of the same name already exists, this function will overwrite it.
 Example: 
`setVariable(context, "foo", 1, "the foo") attaches a variable named "foo", with value set to 1 and the description set to "the foo", on the context.`

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Can be any value, including an array or map with many elements.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: A string describing the use or purpose of the variable.

symbols.name: getVariable
symbols.description:
Retrieve a variable attached to the context by name. Throws an exception if variable by the given name is not found.
 Example: 
`getVariable(context, "foo") returns the value assigned to a previously-set variable named "foo".`Variables on a context can also be accessed within a Part Studio using `#` syntax (e.g. `#foo`) inside any parameter which allows an expression.

symbols.name: getVariable
symbols.signature: (context is Context, name is string, defaultValue)
symbols.description:
Retrieve a variable attached to the context by name. If variable by the given name is not found, returns `defaultValue`
 Example: 
`getVariable(context, "foo", {}) returns the value assigned to a previously-set variable named "foo". If not found returns empty map.`

symbols.name: libraryLanguageVersion
symbols.description: Returns the language version of the library. Note: this function calls `@getLanguageVersion` internally, but if you call `@getLanguageVersion` directly, you may get a different result. That is because `@getLanguageVersion` returns the language version of the module making the call (which, for a module in std will coincide with the version of std.)

geomOperations.fs.name: geomOperations
geomOperations.fs.description: Operations are the basic modeling primitives of FeatureScript. Operations can do extrusion, filleting, transforms, etc. An operation takes a context, an id, and a definition and modifies the context in accordance with the definition. The modifications can be referenced by the passed-in id. Operations return `undefined` but can fail by throwing an error or they can report warnings or infos. The status can be read with the getFeature... functions in error.fs.When an operation parameter that requires one entity receives a query that resolves to multiple entities, it takes the first resolved entity.The geomOperations.fs module contains wrappers around built-in Onshape operations and no actual logic.

symbols.name: opMoveCurveBoundary
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Trims or extends a wire body to an entity or by a distance.

subfields.name: wires
subfields.description: The wire bodies to modify.

subfields.name: moveBoundaryType
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to trim or extend. Default is `TRIM`.

subfields.name: trimTo
subfields.description: Required if `moveBoundaryType` is `TRIM`.Single entity to trim `wires` to.

subfields.name: endCondition
subfields.description: OptionalIf `moveBoundaryType` is `EXTEND` defines whether to extend `wires` by a distance of to an entity. Default is `BLIND`.

subfields.name: extensionDistance
subfields.description: Required if `endCondition` is `BLIND`Distance to extend `wires`.

subfields.name: extendTo
subfields.description: Required if `endCondition` is `BLIND`Single entity to extend `wires` to.

subfields.name: extensionShape
subfields.description: OptionalSpecifies how to transition into the curve extensions. Default is `SOFT`.

subfields.name: helpPoint
subfields.description: Required if `endCondition` is `BLIND`Specifies vertex used to choose a solution. If this is not provided, the closest vertex to the bounding entity will be used.

subfields.name: flipHeuristics
subfields.description: OptionalIf `true`, will trim or extend from the opposite end of `wires`. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opBodyDraft
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Apply draft to a part by adding material. The material is added between reference edges and a parting object. These reference edges can be supplied directly, or they can be inferred from face or part queries.

subfields.name: selectionType
subfields.description: OptionalTopology class for input. Default is `EDGES`

subfields.name: topEdges
subfields.description: Required if `selectionType` is `EDGES`Edges to draft above parting entity.

subfields.name: bottomEdges
subfields.requiredIf: selectionType is EDGES and bothSides is true
subfields.description: Required if `selectionType` is `EDGES` and `bothSides` is `true`Edges to draft with `angleBelow`.

subfields.name: faces
subfields.description: Required if `selectionType` is `FACES`Faces to draft. This will split the face with isoclines those for the draft. Additionally, any existing edges bounding `faces` will be used if they mark a transition from steep faces to non-steep faces for the given pull direction and draft angle.

subfields.name: bodies
subfields.description: Required if `selectionType` is `PARTS`Parts to draft. This is equivalent to supplying all faces of the part in `faces`.

subfields.name: excludeFaces
subfields.description: OptionalFaces of `bodies` to exclude from drafting.

subfields.name: angle
subfields.description: The draft angle above the parting object.

subfields.name: bothSides
subfields.description: OptionalIf `true`, draft on both sides of the parting object. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: pullDirection
subfields.description: The pull direction.

subfields.name: draftOnSelf
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, then using the drafted part as the parting object. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: partingObject
subfields.description: Required if `draftOnSelf` is `false`A surface from surfaceGeometry.fs or a query to a face or sheet body.

subfields.name: matchFacesAtParting
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, then additional material will be added to make the top and bottom of the draft align. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: matchFaceType
subfields.description: OptionalHow to add material for `matchFacesAtParting` Default is `REFERENCE_EDGE`.

subfields.name: cornerType
subfields.description: OptionalThe corner treatment to apply. Default is `EXTEND`.

subfields.name: concaveRepair
subfields.description: OptionalHow to resolve intersecting draft faces. Default is `NONE`.

subfields.name: concaveRepairRadius
subfields.description: Required if `concaveRepair` is `RADIUS` or `MIX`.The radius for intersection repair.

subfields.name: keepMaterial
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, an attempt will be made to keep the regions of the part protruding from the tapered faces. Default is false.

subfields.name: showRefs
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, then debug data will be generated to show the parting surface and draft edges.

symbols.name: opBoolean
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description:
Performs a boolean operation on multiple solid and surface bodies. 
 See also: `processNewBodyIfNeeded` for merging new solids. 
`joinSurfaceBodiesWithAutoMatching` for merging new surfaces.

subfields.name: tools
subfields.description: The tool bodies.

subfields.name: targets
subfields.requiredIf: OperationType is SUBTRACTION or SUBTRACT_COMPLEMENT, or if targetsAndToolsNeedGrouping is true.
subfields.description: Required if `OperationType` is `SUBTRACTION` or `SUBTRACT_COMPLEMENT`, or if `targetsAndToolsNeedGrouping` is true.The target bodies.

subfields.name: operationType
subfields.description:
The boolean operation to perform. 
 Example: 
`BooleanOperationType.UNION will merge any tool bodies that intersect or abut. All tool bodies have to be of the same type (solid or surface). When operating on surfaces, surfaces must have coincident or overlapping edges. When several bodies merge, the identity of the tool that appears earliest in the query is preserved (in particular, body color and body name are taken from it).` Example: 
`BooleanOperationType.SUBTRACTION will remove the union of all tools bodies from every target body. All tool bodies must be solid bodies. Target bodies could be either solids or surfaces.` Example: 
`BooleanOperationType.INTERSECTION will create the intersection of all tool bodies. All bodies must be solid bodies.` Example: 
`BooleanOperationType.SUBTRACT_COMPLEMENT will remove the complement of the union of all tool bodies from every target body. All tool bodies must be solid bodies. Target bodies could be either solids or surfaces.`

subfields.name: targetsAndToolsNeedGrouping
subfields.description: OptionalThis option is for adjusting the behavior to be more suitable for doing the boolean as part of a body-creating feature (such as extrude). Default is `false`.

subfields.name: keepTools
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, the tools do not get consumed by the operation. Default is false.

subfields.name: makeSolid
subfields.description: OptionalIn case of surface union try to join surfaces into a solid. Default is false.

symbols.name: opBoundarySurface
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a boundary surface fitting two ordered sets of profiles.

subfields.name: uProfileSubqueries
subfields.description:
An ordered array of two or fewer queries for the profiles in the u direction. These can be edges or wire bodies. 
 Example: 
`[ profileQuery1, profileQuery2 ]`

subfields.name: vProfileSubqueries
subfields.description:
OptionalAn ordered array of two or fewer queries for the profiles in the v direction. These can be edges or wire bodies. 
 Example: 
`[ profileQuery1, profileQuery2 ]`

subfields.name: uDerivativeInfo
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps that contain shape constraints at start and end profiles. Each map entry is required to have a profileIndex that refers to the affected profile. Optional fields include a vector to match surface tangent to, a magnitude, and booleans for matching tangents or curvature derived from faces adjacent to affected profile. 
 Example: 
`[ { "profileIndex" : 0, "vector" : vector(1, 0, 0), "magnitude" : 2., "tangentToPlane" : true}, { "profileIndex" : 1, "adjacentFaces" : qFaces } ] The first map would constrain the resulting boundary surface at the first u profile to be tangent to plane with normal vector(1,0,0) and magnitude 2. The second map constrains the boundary surface at the second u profile to match tangents of faces defined by the query qFaces.`

subfields.name: vDerivativeInfo
subfields.description: OptionalAn array of maps analogous to uDerivativeInfo, but for v profiles.

subfields.name: showIsocurves
subfields.description: OptionalShow graphical representation of a subset of isoparameteric curves on each face of the created boundary surface. Default `false`.

subfields.name: curveCount
subfields.description: OptionalWhen `showIsocurves` is `true`, the number of curves to draw in each direction of each face's grid. Default `10`.

symbols.name: opCreateBSplineCurve
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Generates a wire body given a `BSplineCurve` definition. The spline must have dimension of 3 and be G1-continuous.

subfields.name: bSplineCurve
subfields.description: The definition of the spline.

symbols.name: opCreateBSplineSurface
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Generates a sheet body given a `BSplineSurface` definition. The spline must have dimension of 3 and be G1-continuous.

subfields.name: bSplineSurface
subfields.description: The definition of the spline surface.

subfields.name: boundaryBSplineCurves
subfields.description: OptionalAn array of `BSplineCurve`s defined in the parameter space of `bSplineSurface` to use as the boundary of the created sheet body. The boundary must form a single closed loop on the surface. The `dimension` of each curve must be `2`, as the boundaries are being defined in UV space of the created surface. If no boundary is supplied, the created sheet body will cover the full parameter range of `bSplineSurface`.

subfields.name: makeSolid
subfields.description: OptionalWhen set to `true`, the operation will produce a solid instead of a sheet if the surface encloses a region. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opCreateCompositePart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Create a composite part.

subfields.name: bodies
subfields.description: Bodies from which to create the composite part. .

subfields.name: closed
subfields.description: OptionalA `closed` composite part consumes its constituent bodies, so that they are not available interactively for individual selection. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opModifyCompositePart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Modifies a composite part.

subfields.name: composite
subfields.description: Existing composite part to modify.

subfields.name: toAdd
subfields.description: Bodies to add to the composite part.

subfields.name: toRemove
subfields.description: Bodies to remove from the composite part.

symbols.name: opChamfer
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Adds a chamfer to given edges and faces.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Edges and faces to chamfer.

subfields.name: chamferType
subfields.description:
Determines where the new edges of the chamfer are positioned. 
 Example: 
`ChamferType.EQUAL_OFFSETS places both new edges width away from each original edge.` Example: 
`ChamferType.TWO_OFFSETS places edges width1 and width2 away.` Example: 
`ChamferType.OFFSET_ANGLE places one edge width away, and chamfers at an angle from that edge.`

subfields.name: width
subfields.description:
Required if `chamferType` is `EQUAL_OFFSETS` or `OFFSET_ANGLE`. Example: 
`0.2 * inch`

subfields.name: width1
subfields.description: Required if `chamferType` is `TWO_OFFSETS`.

subfields.name: width2
subfields.description: Required if `chamferType` is `TWO_OFFSETS`.

subfields.name: angle
subfields.description: Required if `chamferType` is `OFFSET_ANGLE`.

subfields.name: oppositeDirection
subfields.description: Required if `chamferType` is `OFFSET_ANGLE` or `TWO_OFFSETS`.`true` to reverse the two edges.

subfields.name: tangentPropagation
subfields.description: Optional`true` to propagate the chamfer along edges tangent to those passed in. Defaults to `false`.

symbols.name: opConstrainedSurface
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a constrained surface from the points.

subfields.name: points
subfields.description: An array of maps containing: `point` (Vector) : The position of the point we want the surface to pass through. `normal` (Vector) (Optional) : The direction of the normal of the surface at this point.

subfields.name: tolerance
subfields.description: OptionalThe distance the surface is allowed to deviate from the given points. Default is `1e-6 meter`.

subfields.name: smooth
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, the surface will be optimized for smoothness. If false, it will be optimized for performance. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: references
subfields.description: OptionalFor Onshape internal use.

symbols.name: CurveOnFaceDefinition
symbols.description: Describes a set of isoparametric curves on a face.

subfields.name: face
subfields.description: Face the curves are meant to lie on.

subfields.name: creationType
subfields.description: Determines the type of curves. Currently supports isoparameter curves only in primary or secondary directions, either equally spaced or defined by a parameter array.

subfields.name: names
subfields.description: An array of distinct non-empty strings to identify the curves created.

subfields.name: nCurves
subfields.description: Required if `creationType` is `DIR1_AUTO_SPACED_ISO` or `DIR2_AUTO_SPACED_ISO`Number of curves.

subfields.name: parameters
subfields.description: Required if `creationType` is `DIR1_ISO` or `DIR2_ISO`Parameters to create curves at.

symbols.name: canBeCurveOnFaceDefinition
symbols.description: Typecheck for `CurveOnFaceDefinition`

symbols.name: curveOnFaceDefinition
symbols.signature: (face is Query, creationType is FaceCurveCreationType, names is array, nCurves is number)
symbols.description: Returns a new `CurveOnFaceDefinition`.

symbols.name: curveOnFaceDefinition
symbols.signature: (face is Query, creationType is FaceCurveCreationType, names is array, parameters is array)

symbols.name: opCreateCurvesOnFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Generates isoparametric curves of faces. That is, for each specified surface parameter value, creates a new wire body following the curve which keeps the surface parameter at that constant value.

subfields.name: curveDefinition
subfields.description: An array of `CurveOnFaceDefinition`s that describe group of curves per face.

subfields.name: showCurves
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to display isoparameteric curves in color in the preview.

subfields.name: skipTrim
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to skip trimming of curves by the face boundaries. Lines and arcs are always trimmed.

subfields.name: useFaceParameter
subfields.description: OptionalFor Onshape internal use.

symbols.name: opDeleteBodies
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Deletes bodies from the context.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Entities to delete. Passing in entities other than bodies deletes their owning bodies.

symbols.name: opDeleteFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: This is a direct editing operation that attempts to delete faces of a solid body and extend other faces to fill the hole.

subfields.name: deleteFaces
subfields.description: Faces to delete.

subfields.name: includeFillet
subfields.description: `true` to also delete fillets adjacent to the input faces.

subfields.name: capVoid
subfields.description: If `capVoid` is `true` and the deleted face cannot be filled by extending the surrounding faces, will attempt to replace the face with a planar face.

subfields.name: leaveOpen
subfields.description: OptionalIf `leaveOpen` is `true` the void from deleting faces is left open, potentially creating a surface out of a solid body. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opEnclose
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Takes in a set of bodies and faces and creates solid bodies for the enclosed regions.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Bodies and faces for enclosure.

symbols.name: opDraft
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Applies a given draft angle to faces.

subfields.name: draftType
subfields.description:
Specifies a reference surface (e.g. a neutral plane) or reference entity draft. 
 Example: 
`DraftType.REFERENCE_SURFACE for a reference surface draft`

subfields.name: draftFaces
subfields.description: Required if `draftType` is `REFERENCE_SURFACE` The faces to draft for a `REFERENCE_SURFACE` draft.

subfields.name: referenceSurface
subfields.description: Required if `draftType` is `REFERENCE_SURFACE` A face or plane that defines the neutral surface for a `REFERENCE_SURFACE` draft. `draftFaces` will remain unchanged where they intersect `referenceSurface`. Can be either a `Query` or a `Plane`.

subfields.name: referenceEntityDraftOptions
subfields.description: Required if `draftType` is `REFERENCE_ENTITY` An array of maps of the form ("face", "references", "angle"). "face" should be a `Query` for exactly one face. "references" should be a `Query` for at least one edge attached to the face. The "face" will be drafted while the geometry of the "references" remains unchanged. "angle" is an optional `ValueWithUnits` parameter between -89.9 and 89.9 degrees which overrides the default `angle` parameter.

subfields.name: pullVec
subfields.description:
The 3d direction relative to which the draft is applied. 
 Example: 
`vector(0, 0, 1) .`

subfields.name: angle
subfields.description:
The draft angle, must be between 0 and 89.9 degrees. 
 Example: 
`3 * degree`

subfields.name: tangentPropagation
subfields.description: OptionalFor a `REFERENCE_SURFACE` draft, `true` to propagate draft across tangent faces. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: referenceEntityPropagation
subfields.description: OptionalFor a `REFERENCE_ENTITY` draft, `true` to collect new reference entities and faces by pulling in edges connected to the specified reference edges. Connected edges on the same face or on tangent connected faces will be pulled in. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: reFillet
subfields.description: Optional`true` to attempt to defillet draft faces before the draft and reapply the fillets after. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opExtractWires
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Generates wire bodies from the supplied edges. If the edges are disjoint multiple wires will be returned. If the edges overlap or cross, or more than two meet at a point, the function will fail.

subfields.name: edges
subfields.description: The edges to be extracted.

symbols.name: opExtrude
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Extrudes one or more edges or faces in a given direction with one or two end conditions. Faces get extruded into solid bodies and edges get extruded into sheet bodies.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Edges and faces to extrude.

subfields.name: direction
subfields.description:
The 3d direction in which to extrude. 
 Example: 
`evOwnerSketchPlane(context, {"entity" : entities}).normal to extrude perpendicular to the owner sketch` Example: 
`evPlane(context, {"face" : entities}).normal to extrude perpendicular to the first planar entity`

subfields.name: endBound
subfields.description:
The type of bound at the end of the extrusion. Cannot be `SYMMETRIC` or `UP_TO_VERTEX`. 
 Example: 
`BoundingType.BLIND`

subfields.name: endDepth
subfields.description:
Required if `endBound` is `BLIND`.How far from the `entities` to extrude. 
 Example: 
`1 * inch`

subfields.name: endBoundEntity
subfields.description: Required if `endBound` is `UP_TO_SURFACE` or `UP_TO_BODY`.The face or body that provides the bound.

subfields.name: endTranslationalOffset
subfields.description: OptionalThe translational offset between the extrude end cap and the end bound entity. The direction vector for this is the same as `direction`: negative values pull the end cap away from the bound entity when `endDepth` is positive. `endOffset` will only have an effect if `endBound` is `UP_TO_SURFACE`, `UP_TO_BODY`, or `UP_TO_NEXT`.

subfields.name: startBound
subfields.description: OptionalThe type of start bound. Default is for the extrude to start at `entities`. Cannot be `SYMMETRIC` or `UP_TO_VERTEX`.

subfields.name: isStartBoundOpposite
subfields.description: Required if is `UP_TO_SURFACE`, `UP_TO_BODY`, or `UP_TO_NEXT`.Whether the `startBound` extends in the opposite direction from the profile as the `endBound`. Defaults to `true` if not supplied.

subfields.name: startDepth
subfields.description: Required if `startBound` is `BLIND`.How far from the `entities` to start the extrude. The direction vector for this is the negative of `direction`: positive values make the extrusion longer when `endDepth` is positive.

subfields.name: startBoundEntity
subfields.description: Required if `startBound` is `UP_TO_SURFACE` or `UP_TO_BODY`.The face or body that provides the bound.

subfields.name: startTranslationalOffset
subfields.description: OptionalThe translational offset between the extrude start cap and the start bound entity. The direction vector for this is the negative of `direction`: negative values pull the end cap away from the bound entity when `startDepth` is positive. `startOffset` will only have an effect if `startBound` is `UP_TO_SURFACE`, `UP_TO_BODY`, or `UP_TO_NEXT`.

symbols.name: opFaceBlend
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Performs a face blend between two walls of faces.

subfields.name: side1
subfields.description: First set of faces, must belong to the same body.

subfields.name: side2
subfields.description: Second set of faces, must belong to the same body.

subfields.name: flipSide1Normal
subfields.description: Wether to flip side 1's normal.

subfields.name: flipSide2Normal
subfields.description: Wether to flip side 2's normal.

subfields.name: propagation
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to propagate the blend.

subfields.name: propagationType
subfields.description: OptionalHow to propagate the blend. FaceBlendPropagation.TANGENT will propagate over tangent faces. FaceBlendPropagation.ADJACENT will propagate over all adjacent faces. FaceBlendPropagation.CUSTOM allows specification of a maximum angle between faces for propagation. Defaults is FaceBlendPropagation.TANGENT.

subfields.name: propagationAngle
subfields.description: OptionalMaximum angle between faces for propagation. Used if propagationType is FaceBlendPropagation.CUSTOM. Default is 0 * radian.

subfields.name: crossSection
subfields.description: The cross section type of the blend.

subfields.name: spine
subfields.description: Required if `crossSection` is `DISC`.The spine of the blend. The blend's cross section will be orthogonal to the spine.

subfields.name: blendControlType
subfields.description: Whether the blend is controled by a constant radius or a constant width.

subfields.name: crossSectionShape
subfields.description: What shape the cross section of the blend will be.

subfields.name: radius
subfields.description: Required if `blendControlType` is `RADIUS`.The radius of the cross section.

subfields.name: width
subfields.description: Required if `blendControlType` is `WIDTH`.The width of the blend.

subfields.name: asymmetric
subfields.description: OptionalWether the radius or width is the same on each wall.

subfields.name: secondRadius
subfields.requiredIf: blendControlType is RADIUS and asymmetric is true.
subfields.description: Required if `blendControlType` is `RADIUS` and `asymmetric` is `true`.The radius of the cross section from side 2.

subfields.name: widthRatio
subfields.requiredIf: blendControlType is WIDTH and asymmetric is true.
subfields.description: Required if `blendControlType` is `WIDTH` and `asymmetric` is `true`.How the blend will be divided between the walls. If widthRatio < 1, it will be wider towards side 1. If widthRatio > 1, it will be wider towards side 2.

subfields.name: rho
subfields.description: Required if `crossSectionShape` is `CONIC`Parameter of the conic cross section shape.

subfields.name: magnitude
subfields.description: Required if `crossSectionShape` is `CURVATURE`Parameter of the curvature cross section shape.

subfields.name: tangentHoldLines
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to use the content of `tangentEdges` and `inverseTangentEdges`.

subfields.name: tangentEdges
subfields.description: OptionalEdges to use as tangent hold lines.

subfields.name: inverseTangentEdges
subfields.description: OptionalEdges to use as inverse tangent hold lines.

subfields.name: conicHoldLines
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to use the content of `conicEdges` and `inverseConicEdges`.

subfields.name: conicEdges
subfields.description: OptionalEdges to use as conic hold lines.

subfields.name: inverseConicEdges
subfields.description: OptionalEdges to use as inverse conic hold lines.

subfields.name: hasCliffEdges
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to use the content of `cliffEdges`.

subfields.name: cliffEdges
subfields.description: OptionalEdges to use as cliff edges.

subfields.name: hasCaps
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to use the content of `caps`.

subfields.name: caps
subfields.description: OptionalEntities and flip flags to use as caps. Each map should contain an `entity` Query element and a `flip` boolean element.

subfields.name: hasLimits
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to use the content of `limitPlane1`, `limitPlane2`, `faceLimits` and `edgeLimit`.

subfields.name: limitPlane1
subfields.description: OptionalFirst plane to limit the blend.

subfields.name: limitPlane1Flip
subfields.description: Whether to flip the first plane normal. Defines which side of the plane the blend will be.

subfields.name: limitPlane2
subfields.description: OptionalSecond plane to limit the blend.

subfields.name: limitPlane2Flip
subfields.description: Whether to flip the first plane normal. Defines which side of the plane the blend will be.

subfields.name: faceLimits
subfields.description: OptionalFaces to use as limits to the blend.

subfields.name: edgeLimit
subfields.description: OptionalEdges and adjacent faces to use as limits. Each map should contain an `edge` Query element and an `edgeLimitSide` query element.

subfields.name: hasHelpPoint
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to use the content of `helpPoint`.

subfields.name: helpPoint
subfields.description: OptionalVertex or mate connector to use as help point. In case the blend parameters create several blends, only the blend closest to the help point will be kept.

subfields.name: trim
subfields.description: OptionalHow to trim the blend.

subfields.name: detach
subfields.description: OptionalWhether not to attach the blend(s) to the sides, creating new sheet bodies.

subfields.name: showIsocurves
subfields.description: OptionalShow graphical representation of a subset of isoparameteric curves of the created surface. Default `false`.

subfields.name: curveCount
subfields.description: OptionalWhen `showIsocurves` is `true`, the number of curves to draw in each direction of the grid. Default `10`.

symbols.name: opFillet
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: For edges, performs a fillet on the edge. For faces, performs a fillet on all edges adjacent to the face.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Edges and faces to fillet.

subfields.name: radius
subfields.description:
The fillet radius. 
 Example: 
`1 * inch`

subfields.name: tangentPropagation
subfields.description: Optional`true` to propagate the fillet along edges tangent to those passed in. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: crossSection
subfields.description: OptionalFillet cross section. One of `CIRCULAR`, `CONIC`, `CURVATURE`. Default is `CIRCULAR`.

subfields.name: rho
subfields.description:
Required if `crossSection` is `CONIC`.A number between 0 and 1, specifying the Rho value of a conic fillet 
 Example: 
`0.01 creates a flat, nearly-chamfered shape.` Example: 
`0.99 creates a pointed, nearly-unchanged shape.`

subfields.name: magnitude
subfields.description: Required if `crossSection` is `CURVATURE`.A number between 0 and 1, specifying the magnitude of curvature match.

subfields.name: partialFilletBounds
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps representing the boundaries of a partial fillet. Each map should contain a `boundaryEdge` query, a `boundaryParameter` value, a `isFlipped` boolean 
 Example: 
`[{ "boundaryEdge" : edgeQuery0, "boundaryParameter" : 0.3, "isFlipped" : false }, { "boundaryEdge" : edgeQuery1, "boundaryParameter" : 0.6, "isFlipped" : true }]`

subfields.name: isVariable
subfields.description: OptionalFillet controls can be varied at vertices via `vertexSettings`. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: vertexSettings
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps representing fillet settings at specified vertices. Each map should contain a `vertex` query, a `vertexRadius` value, a `variableMagnitude` if the `crossSection` is `FilletCrossSection.CURVATURE`, and a `variableRho` if the `crossSection` is `FilletCrossSection.CONIC`. 
 Example: 
`[{ "vertex" : vertexQuery0, "vertexRadius" : 1 * inch, "variableRho" : 0.2 }, { "vertex" : vertexQuery1, "vertexRadius" : 2 * inch, "variableRho" : 0.8 }]`

subfields.name: pointOnEdgeSettings
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps representing fillet settings at specified points on edges. Each map should contain an `edge` query, an `edgeParameter` value, a `pointOnEdgeRadius` value, a `pointOnEdgeVariablMagnitude` if the `crossSection` is `FilletCrossSection.CURVATURE`, and a `pointOnEdgeVariableRho` if the `crossSection` is `FilletCrossSection.CONIC`. 
 Example: 
`[{ "edge" : edgeQuery0, "edgeParameter" : 0.3, "pointOnEdgeRadius" : 1 * inch }, { "edge" : edgeQuery1, "edgeParameter" : 0.6, "pointOnEdgeRadius" : 2 * inch }]`

subfields.name: smoothTransition
subfields.description: Required if `isVariable` is `true` Whether to create a smoother transition between each vertex.

subfields.name: allowEdgeOverflow
subfields.description: OptionalAllow `opFillet` to modify nearby edges to maintain the fillet profile. Default is `true`.

subfields.name: keepEdges
subfields.description: OptionalEdges you do not want `opFillet` to modify if `allowEdgeOverflow` is `true`.

subfields.name: smoothCorners
subfields.description: OptionalAllow `opFillet` to smooth all suitable corners and prevent creation of sharp edges. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: smoothCornerExceptions
subfields.description: OptionalVertices you do not want `opFillet` to smooth if `smoothCorners` is `true`.

subfields.name: createDetachedSurface
subfields.description: OptionalOperation does not modify the body of the selected edges, but results in surface geometry of fillet. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opFillSurface
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Generates a surface body from supplied boundary and internal constraints. The boundaries are defined as edge queries for each continuity constraint. The internal constraints may be defined as a set of support vertices.

subfields.name: edgesG0
subfields.description: The edges with position constraints.

subfields.name: edgesG1
subfields.description: The edges with tangency constraints.

subfields.name: edgesG2
subfields.description: The edges with curvature constraints.

subfields.name: guideVertices
subfields.description: The vertices the resulting surface is expected to interpolate.

subfields.name: showIsocurves
subfields.description: OptionalShow graphical representation of a subset of isoparameteric curves of the created surface. Default `false`.

subfields.name: curveCount
subfields.description: OptionalWhen `showIsocurves` is `true`, the number of curves to draw in each direction of the grid. Default `10`.

symbols.name: opFitSpline
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a 3D cubic spline curve through an array of 3D points.

subfields.name: points
subfields.description:
An array of `Vector`s with length units for the spline to interpolate. If the first point is the same as the last point, the spline is closed. 
 Example: 

```
[
    vector( 1,  1,  1) * inch,
    vector(-1,  1, -1) * inch,
    vector( 1, -1, -1) * inch,
    vector(-1, -1,  1) * inch,
    vector( 1,  1,  1) * inch
]
```

subfields.name: parameters
subfields.description: OptionalAn array of doubles, parameters corresponding to the points.

subfields.name: startDerivative
subfields.description: OptionalA `Vector` with length units that specifies the derivative at the start of the resulting spline (according to the `arcLengthParameterization` set to `false`).

subfields.name: endDerivative
subfields.description: OptionalA `Vector` with length units that specifies the derivative at the end of the resulting spline. Ignored if spline is closed.

subfields.name: start2ndDerivative
subfields.description: OptionalA `Vector` with length units that specifies the second derivative at the start of the resulting spline. Ignored if spline is closed, or if `startDerivative` is not defined

subfields.name: end2ndDerivative
subfields.description: OptionalA `Vector` with length units that specifies the second derivative at the end of the resulting spline. Ignored if spline is closed, or if `endDerivative` is not defined

subfields.name: derivatives
subfields.description: OptionalA map of derivatives at non-end points. Entries should be `index : derivative`, where `index` is an integer between 1 and `size(points) - 2` and `derivative` is a `Vector` that specifies the derivative at `points[index]`.

symbols.name: opFlipOrientation
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Reverses the orientation of sheet bodies.

subfields.name: bodies
subfields.description: Sheet bodies whose orientation should be flipped.

symbols.name: opFullRoundFillet
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a full round fillet, replacing the center face(s) with circular profile face(s) of varying radius, joining the selected side faces.

subfields.name: side1Face
subfields.description: A face on one side of the blend.

subfields.name: side2Face
subfields.description: A face on another side of the blend.

subfields.name: centerFaces
subfields.description: The face(s) to be replaced.

subfields.name: tangentPropagation
subfields.description: Optional`true` to propagate the fillet across side face tangencies. Default is `true`.

symbols.name: opHelix
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a helical and possibly spiral curve.

subfields.name: direction
subfields.description:
The direction of the helix axis. 
 Example: 
`vector(0, 0, 1)`

subfields.name: axisStart
subfields.description:
A point on the helix axis. 
 Example: 
`vector(0, 0, 0) * inch`

subfields.name: startPoint
subfields.description:
The start point of the infinite helix. Must be off the axis. This is the point at which the created curve would actually start if the first number of `interval` is 0. 
 Example: 
`vector(1, 0, 0) * inch`

subfields.name: interval
subfields.description:
An array of two numbers denoting the interval of the helix in terms of revolution counts. 
 Example: 
`[0, 10] will create a curve with 10 revolutions.`

subfields.name: clockwise
subfields.description:
Example: 
`true if this is a clockwise helix when viewed along direction.`

subfields.name: helicalPitch
subfields.description:
Distance along the axis between successive revolutions. 
 Example: 
`0.1 * inch will create a helix with 10 revolutions per inch.` Example: 
`0 * inch produces a planar Archimedean spiral.`

subfields.name: spiralPitch
subfields.description:
Change in radius between successive revolutions. 
 Example: 
`0 * inch produces a helix that lies on a cylinder.`

symbols.name: opHole
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates hole tools referencing a set of targets, optionally subtracting the tools from the targets. If some tools cannot be built, the operation will still succeed and indicate in its return value which holes failed to build. If no tools can be built, the operation will fail.

subfields.name: holeDefinition
subfields.description:
The definition of the shape of the desired holes. 
 Example: 
`holeDefinition([holeProfile(HolePositionReference.AXIS_POINT, 0 * inch, 0.1 * inch), holeProfile(HolePositionReference.AXIS_POINT, 1 * inch, 0 * inch)])`

subfields.name: axes
subfields.description:
An array of `Line`s each of whose `origin` represents the start position of a hole, and whose `direction` represents the drill direction of the hole. 
 Example: 
`[line(vector(-1, -1, 0) * inch, vector(0, 0, -1)), line(vector(1, 1, 0) * inch, vector(0, 0, -1))]`

subfields.name: identities
subfields.description: OptionalAn array of queries, one per axis in `axes`, used to disambiguate each of the created holes. Each query should resolve to exactly one entity. Providing this information does not change the geometric outcome, but stabilizes references to the holes with respect to upstream changes to the model.

subfields.name: targets
subfields.requiredIf: holeDefinition contains any profiles that do not reference HolePositionReference.AXIS_POINT, or if subtractFromTargets is true
subfields.description: Required if `holeDefinition` contains any `profiles` that do not reference `HolePositionReference.AXIS_POINT`, or if `subtractFromTargets` is `true` A set of bodies to target. The shape of the produced holes is dependent on the shape of these targets (as specified in the supplied `HoleDefinition`), so the full set of targeted bodies should always be supplied, even if `subtractFromTargets` is `false`.

subfields.name: subtractFromTargets
subfields.description: Optional`true` if the hole geometry should be subtracted from the targets. `false` if the targets should not be modified, and the hole tools should be outputted as new solid bodies. Default is `true`. To subtract from a subset of targets, set this to `true` and supply a set of excluded targets as `targetsToExcludeFromSubtraction`. Removing the set of excluded targets from `targets` instead of using `targetsToExcludeFromSubtraction` is not the correct way to call this interface, and may result in the shape of the hole changing.

subfields.name: targetsToExcludeFromSubtraction
subfields.description: OptionalIf supplied and `subtractFromTargets` is `true`, the given targets are excluded from the subtraction. Ignored if `subtractFromTargets` is `false`

subfields.name: keepTools
subfields.description: OptionalIf `subtractFromTargets` is `true`, controls whether the hole tools should be outputted as new solid bodies. Default is `false`. Ignored if `subtractFromTargets` is `false`; in that case hole tools are always outputted as new solid bodies.

symbols.name: opImportForeign
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Brings foreign geometry into the context. This function is used for importing uploaded parts.

subfields.name: blobData
subfields.description: Reference to a blob element hosting uploaded CAD data.

subfields.name: flatten
subfields.description: OptionalWhether to flatten assemblies; defaults to false.

subfields.name: yAxisIsUp
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, the y axis in the import maps to the z axis and z maps to -y. If false (default), the coordinates are unchanged.

subfields.name: isModifiable
subfields.description: OptionalWhether the imported data is modifiable (default) or not.

symbols.name: opCreateIsocline
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates wires corresponding to the given `faces`'s isocline edges. Each isocline follows a path along a face with a constant `angle` in the (-90, 90) degree range (e.g., lines of latitude on a sphere). This `angle` is the face tangent plane's angle with respect to the `direction` with its sign determined by the dot product of `direction` and the face normal, and is analogous to the angle used in draft analysis. Depending on the face geometry, there may be zero, one, or multiple isoclines on each face.

subfields.name: faces
subfields.description: The faces on which to imprint isoclines.

subfields.name: direction
subfields.description: A reference direction.

subfields.name: angle
subfields.description: The isocline angle with respect to the direction in the (-90, 90) degree range.

symbols.name: opLoft
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a surface or solid loft fitting an ordered set of profiles, optionally constrained by guide curves.

subfields.name: profileSubqueries
subfields.description:
An ordered array of queries for the profiles. For a solid loft, these must be sheet bodies, faces, or vertices. For a surface loft, these could be wire bodies, sheet bodies, faces, edges, or vertices. 
 Example: 
`[ profileQuery1, profileQuery2 ]`

subfields.name: guideSubqueries
subfields.description: OptionalAn array of queries for guide curves. Each guide curve should intersect each profile once.

subfields.name: connections
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps to define multiple profile alignments. Each connection map should contain:
(1) connectionEntities query describing an array of vertices or edges (one per profile),
(2) connectionEdges an array of individual queries for edges in connectionEntities. The order of individual edge queries should be synchronized with connectionEdgeParameters.
(3) connectionEdgeParameters array - an ordered and synchronized array of parameters on edges in connectionEdgeQueries 
 Example: 
`[ {"connectionEntities" : qVertexAndEdge1, "connectionEdges" : [qEdge1], "connectionEdgeParameters" : [0.25]} {"connectionEntities" : qVertexAndEdge2, "connectionEdges" : [qEdge2], "connectionEdgeParameters" : [0.75]}]`

subfields.name: connectionsArcLengthParameterization
subfields.description: OptionalDefaults to false for better performance. Controls interpretation of connectionEdgeParameters. If `evDistance`, `evEdgeTangentLine` etc. are called in conjunction with opLoft the same value should be passed as `arcLengthParameterization` there.

subfields.name: makePeriodic
subfields.description: OptionalDefaults to false. A closed guide creates a periodic loft regardless of this option.

subfields.name: bodyType
subfields.description: OptionalSpecifies a `SOLID` or `SURFACE` loft. Default is `SOLID`.

subfields.name: trimGuidesByProfiles
subfields.description: OptionalIf false (default) use full length of guides. If true restrict resulting surface by the first and last profile. Meaningful only for non-periodic surface loft.

subfields.name: trimProfiles
subfields.description: OptionalIf false (default) use full length of profiles. If true restrict resulting surface by the first and last guide or connection. Meaningful only for surface loft with open profiles.

subfields.name: derivativeInfo
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps that contain shape constraints at start and end profiles. Each map entry is required to have a profileIndex that refers to the affected profile. Optional fields include a vector to match surface tangent to, a magnitude, and booleans for matching tangents or curvature derived from faces adjacent to affected profile. 
 Example: 
`[ { "profileIndex" : 0, "vector" : vector(1, 0, 0), "magnitude" : 2., "tangentToPlane" : true}, { "profileIndex" : 1, "matchCurvature" : true, "adjacentFaces" : qFaces } ] The first map would constrain the resulting loft at the start profile to be tangent to plane with normal vector(1,0,0) and magnitude 2. The second map constrains the loft at the end profile to match the curvature of faces defined by the query qFaces.`

subfields.name: showIsocurves
subfields.description: OptionalShow graphical representation of a subset of isoparameteric curves on each face of the created loft. Default `false`.

subfields.name: curveCount
subfields.description: OptionalWhen `showIsocurves` is `true`, the number of curves to draw in each direction of each face's grid. Default `10`.

subfields.name: loftTopology
subfields.description: OptionalSpecifies topology of lofted body. Default is `MINIMAL`.

subfields.name: addSections
subfields.description: Optional`true` to add a path. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: spine
subfields.description: OptionalQuery to specify the edge for the path.

subfields.name: sectionCount
subfields.description: OptionalNumber of sections to add. Required if `addSections=true`.

symbols.name: opMateConnector
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a mate connector, which represents a coordinate system in the context. Currently it is a special type of point body.

subfields.name: coordSystem
subfields.description: The mate connector coordinate system.

subfields.name: owner
subfields.description: The owner body of the mate connector: when the owner is brought into an assembly, owned mate connectors will be brought in and move rigidly with it. If the query resolves to multiple bodies, the first is taken as the owner.

symbols.name: opMergeContexts
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Bring all of the information from `contextFrom` into `context`. This is used, for example, for the Derived feature.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: The target context.

subfields.name: contextFrom
subfields.description:
The source context. It is rendered unusable by this operation. 
 Example: 
`MyPartStudio::build()`

subfields.name: trackThroughMerge
subfields.description: OptionalArray of queries to map evaluation result in contextFrom to context post-merge

returnInfo.description: Returns array of the same size as trackThroughMerge with evaluation results for each query(array of arrays of transient queries).

symbols.name: opModifyFillet
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: This is a direct editing operation that modifies or deletes fillets.

subfields.name: faces
subfields.description: The fillets to modify.

subfields.name: modifyFilletType
subfields.description: Whether to change the fillet radii or remove them altogether.

subfields.name: radius
subfields.description: Required if `modifyFilletType` is `CHANGE_RADIUS`.The new radius.

subfields.name: reFillet
subfields.description: Required if `modifyFilletType` is `CHANGE_RADIUS`.`true` to reapply adjacent fillets. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opMoveFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: This is a direct editing operation that applies a transform to one or more faces.

subfields.name: moveFaces
subfields.description: The faces to transform.

subfields.name: transform
subfields.description:
The transform to apply to the face. 
 Example: 
`transform(vector(0, 0, 1) * inch) will translate the face 1 inch along the world's z-axis.`

subfields.name: reFillet
subfields.description: Optional`true` to attempt to defillet `moveFaces` prior to the move and reapply the fillet after. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: mergeFaces
subfields.description: Optional`true` to remove redundant edges from moveFaces. Default is `true`.

symbols.name: opOffsetFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: This is a direct editing operation that offsets one or more faces.

subfields.name: moveFaces
subfields.description: The faces to offset.

subfields.name: offsetDistance
subfields.description:
The positive or negative distance by which to offset. 
 Example: 
`0.1 * inch will offset the face 0.1 inches, normal to the face.`

subfields.name: reFillet
subfields.description: Optional`true` to attempt to defillet `moveFaces` prior to the offset and reapply the fillet after. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: mergeFaces
subfields.description: Optional`true` to remove redundant edges from moveFaces. Default is `true`.

symbols.name: opPattern
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Patterns input faces and/or bodies by applying transforms to them. The original faces and bodies are preserved.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Bodies and faces to pattern.

subfields.name: transforms
subfields.description: An array of `Transform`s to apply to `entities`. The transforms do not have to be rigid.

subfields.name: instanceNames

subfields.name: copyPropertiesAndAttributes
subfields.description: OptionalIf true (default), copies properties and attributes to patterned entities.

symbols.name: opPlane
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a construction plane.

subfields.name: plane
subfields.description:
The plane to create. 
 Example: 
`plane(vector(0, 0, 6) * inch, vector(0, 0, 1))`

subfields.name: width
subfields.description: OptionalThe side length of the construction plane, as it is initially displayed.

subfields.name: height
subfields.description: OptionalThe side length of the construction plane, as it is initially displayed.

subfields.name: defaultType
subfields.description: OptionalFor Onshape internal use.

symbols.name: opPolyline
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a polyline passing through an array of 3D points.

subfields.name: points
subfields.description:
An array of `Vector`s with length units corresponding the points of the polyline. If the firstpoint is the same as the last point, the polyline is closed. 
 Example: 

```
[
    vector( 1,  1,  1) * inch,
    vector(-1,  1, -1) * inch,
    vector( 1, -1, -1) * inch,
    vector(-1, -1,  1) * inch,

]
```

subfields.name: bendRadii

A value of `0 * meter` means no bend at the corresponding point. Its size should be `size(points) - 2` for an open polyline, `size(points) - 1` for a closed one.

symbols.name: opDropCurve

subfields.name: targets

subfields.name: projectionType

subfields.name: direction
subfields.description: OptionalThe direction in which to project the curve. Required if projectionType is ProjectionType.DIRECTION.

symbols.name: opIntersectFaces
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Intersect two faces, creating curves appropriately.

subfields.name: tools
subfields.description: First array of faces to intersect.

subfields.name: targets
subfields.description: Second array of faces to intersect.

symbols.name: opSphere
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a sphere.

subfields.name: radius
subfields.description:
The radius of the sphere. 
 Example: 
`1 * inch`

subfields.name: center
subfields.description:
The location of the center of the sphere. 
 Example: 
`vector(1, 1, 1) * inch`

symbols.name: opSplineThroughEdges
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a 3D spline curve representing a sequence of edges. The edges must form a tangent-continuous chain.

subfields.name: edges
subfields.description: Edges to approximate.

symbols.name: opSplitEdges
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Splits an array of edges with an entity or at specified parameters.

subfields.name: edges
subfields.description: Edges to split.

subfields.name: parameters
subfields.description: An array of array of parameters. Each edge gets split at the parameter values at the matching index in this array.

subfields.name: splittingSurface
subfields.description: A sheet body, a construction plane or a face to cut with. Can be either a `Query` or a `Plane`. Either `splittingSurface` or `parameters` must be specified but not both.

subfields.name: arcLengthParameterization
subfields.description: OptionalIf true (default), the parameter returned for edges measures distance along the edge, so `0.5` is the midpoint. If false, use an arbitrary but faster-to-calculate parameterization. Default is `true`.

symbols.name: opPoint
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a construction point (a `BodyType.POINT` with one vertex).

subfields.name: point
subfields.description:

symbols.name: opReplaceFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: This is a direct editing operation that replaces the geometry one or more faces with that of another face or multiple adjacent faces, possibly with an offset.

subfields.name: replaceFaces
subfields.description: The faces whose geometry to replace.

subfields.name: templateFace
subfields.description: The face or multiple adjacent faces whose geometry to use as the replacement.

subfields.name: offset
subfields.description: OptionalThe positive or negative distance by which to offset the resulting face.

subfields.name: oppositeSense
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, flip the surface normal of the resulting face, which may be necessary to match the surface normals of surrounding faces. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: opRevolve
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Revolves edges and faces about an axis to produce sheet and solid bodies. The edges and faces may abut, but not strictly intersect the axis.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: The edges and faces to revolve.

subfields.name: axis
subfields.description:
The axis around which to revolve. 
 Example: 
`line(vector(0, 0, 0) * inch, vector(0, 0, 1))`

subfields.name: angleForward
subfields.description:
The angle where the revolve ends relative to `entities`. Normalized to the range \[0, 2 PI). 
 Example: 
`30 * degree`

subfields.name: angleBack
subfields.description: OptionalThe angle where the revolve starts relative to `entities`. Normalized to the range \[0, 2 PI). If `angleForward == angleBack`, the revolve is a full (360-degree) revolve. Defaults to `0`.

symbols.name: opRuledSurface
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Creates a ruled surface along a set of paths. Direction of ruled surface is specified with either `ruledDirection` or `angleFromFaces`.

subfields.name: path
subfields.description: The edges that form the paths for the ruled surface.

subfields.name: cornerType
subfields.description: OptionalHow corners in the ruled surface are handled. Default is `RuledSurfaceCornerType.SPUN`.

subfields.name: useCubicInterpolation
subfields.description: OptionalRuled surface will use a cubic interpolation if `true`. Otherwise, a linear interpolation will be used. Default is `true`.

subfields.name: width
subfields.description: The width of the ruled surface.

subfields.name: ruledSurfaceType
subfields.description: Specifies how the ruled surface is constructed.

subfields.name: ruledDirection
subfields.description: Required if `ruledSurfaceType` is `ALIGNED_WITH_VECTOR` Ruled surface will be aligned with this vector.

subfields.name: angle
subfields.description: The angle at which the ruled surface meets reference faces or `ruledDirection`. Default is `0`.

subfields.name: referenceFaces
subfields.description: Required if `ruledSurfaceType` is `ANGLE_FROM_FACE` A set of faces from which to measure `angleFromFaces`.

subfields.name: vertex
subfields.description: A vertex on the path where the override is applied.

subfields.name: ruledDirection
subfields.description: If specified, override will specify local direction of ruled surface along this vector.

subfields.name: width
subfields.description: Required if `ruledDirection` != undefined or `angleFromFaces` != undefined Width of ruled surface at this override.

subfields.name: angleFromFaces
subfields.description: If specified, override will specify direction as an angle to reference faces. This is only applicable if angleFromFaces is also specified at the top level.

subfields.name: upToEntity
subfields.description: If specified, override will specify that ruled surface touches upToEntity at override.

symbols.name: opShell

symbols.description: Create a shell of a solid body with uniform thickness. The bodies that are passed in are hollowed, omitting the walls on the `face` entities passed in.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: The faces to shell and solid bodies to hollow.

subfields.name: thickness
subfields.description: The distance by which to shell. Positive means shell outward, and negative means shell inward.

symbols.name: opSplitPart
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Split solid, sheet, and wire bodies with the given sheet body.

subfields.name: targets
subfields.description: The solid, sheet, and wire bodies to split.

subfields.name: tool
subfields.description: A sheet body, a construction plane or a face to cut with. Can be either a `Query` or a `Plane`. If a planar face is passed in, the split will extend the plane infinitely unless `useTrimmed` is `true`.

subfields.name: keepTools
subfields.description: OptionalIf false, the tool is deleted. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: keepType
subfields.description: OptionalControls which pieces to keep. Default is `KEEP_ALL`.

subfields.name: useTrimmed
subfields.description: OptionalIf true, the trimmed face boundaries are used as the tool, rather than the underlying surface. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: SplitFaceResult
symbols.description: Map containing the results of opSplitFace.

subfields.name: splittingEdges
subfields.description: An array of edges where the faces intersect.

symbols.name: opSplitFace
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Split faces with the given edges or faces. Returns an array of edges where the faces intersect. This includes already existing edges as well as edges that were created by opSplitFace.

subfields.name: faceTargets
subfields.description: The faces to split.

subfields.name: edgeTools
subfields.description: OptionalThe edges to cut with.

subfields.name: projectionType
subfields.description: OptionalEdge projection method. Default is `ProjectionType.NORMAL_TO_TARGET`

subfields.name: direction
subfields.requiredIf: edgeTools are present and projectionType is ProjectionType.DIRECTION.
subfields.description: Required if `edgeTools` are present and `projectionType` is `ProjectionType.DIRECTION`.The projection direction.

subfields.name: faceTools
subfields.description: OptionalThe faces to cut with.

subfields.name: bodyTools
subfields.description: OptionalThe sheet or wire bodies to cut with.

subfields.name: keepToolSurfaces
subfields.description: OptionalIf `true`, the `bodyTools` do not get consumed by the operation. Default is `true`.

subfields.name: planeTools
subfields.description: OptionalThese planar faces are treated as infinite, rather than bounded to the face extents.

subfields.name: extendToCompletion
subfields.description: Optionalif `true`, imprinted edges are extended to complete split of faces. Default is `false`.

subfields.name: mutualImprint
subfields.description: Optionalif `true` and only `bodyTools` are supplied target faces are used to split faces of `bodyTools`. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: SplitByIsoclineResult
symbols.description: Map containing the results of splitting faces by their isoclines. Some faces may have been split, others may have been left intact.

subfields.name: nonSteepFaces

subfields.name: boundaryEdges

subfields.name: angle

symbols.name: SplitBySelfShadowResult

subfields.name: bodies

subfields.name: viewDirection

symbols.name: opSweep
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Sweep the given edges and faces along a path resulting in solid and/or sheet bodies.

subfields.name: profiles
subfields.description: Edges and faces to sweep.

subfields.name: path
subfields.description: Edges that comprise the path along which to sweep. The edges can be in any order but must form a connected path.

subfields.name: keepProfileOrientation
subfields.description: OptionalIf `true`, the profile maintains its original orientation as it is swept. If `false` (default), the profile rotates to remain normal to the path.

subfields.name: lockFaces
subfields.description: OptionalKeep profile aligned to the normals of these faces.

subfields.name: lockDirection
subfields.description: OptionalKeep profile perpendicular to this direction.

subfields.name: profileControl
subfields.description:
OptionalDefault is NONE 
 Example: 
`ProfileControlMode.KEEP_ORIENTATION the profile maintains its original orientation as it is swept.` Example: 
`ProfileControlMode.LOCK_DIRECTION the profile is perpendicular to given direction.` Example: 
`ProfileControlMode.LOCK_FACES the profile is aligned to the normals of given faces.`

symbols.name: opTessellatedLoft
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Create a tessellated loft between two edge or vertex profiles.

subfields.name: profileSubqueries
subfields.description: A two element array containing edge or vertex profiles to loft between.

subfields.name: chordalTolerance
subfields.description: The maximum distance a chord can deviate from the path. Default is `0.005 meters`

subfields.name: connections
subfields.description:
OptionalAn array of maps to define multiple profile alignments. Each connection map should contain:
(1) connectionEntities query describing an array of vertices or edges (one per profile),
(2) connectionEdges an array of individual queries for edges in connectionEntities. The order of individual edge queries should be synchronized with connectionEdgeParameters.
(3) connectionEdgeParameters array - an ordered and synchronized array of parameters on edges in connectionEdgeQueries

subfields.name: modelParameters

subfields.name: backThickness

subfields.name: bendRadius

symbols.name: opThicken
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Thicken sheet bodies and faces into solid bodies.

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: The sheet bodies and faces to thicken.

subfields.name: thickness1
subfields.description: The distance by which to thicken in the direction along the normal.

subfields.name: thickness2
subfields.description: The distance by which to thicken in the opposite direction.

subfields.name: keepTools
subfields.description: OptionalDefault is `true`. If `false` the operation will attempt to delete the `entities`. The operation will not delete sheet bodies unless the sheet body or all faces of the sheet body are selected. The operation will not delete sketches or solid bodies.

symbols.name: opTransform
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description: Applies a given transform to one or more bodies. To make transformed copies of bodies, use `opPattern`.

subfields.name: bodies
subfields.description: The bodies to transform.

subfields.name: transform
subfields.description:
The transform to apply. 
 Example: 
`transform(vector(0, 0, 1) * inch) will translate the body 1 inch along the world's z-axis.` Example: 
`rotationAround(myLine, 30 * degree) will rotate around a Line object.` Example: 
`scaleUniformly(factor) will scale uniformly about the origin.` Example: 
`scaleUniformly(factor, point) will scale uniformly about a given point.` Example: 
`toWorld(cSys) will (somewhat counterintuitively) perform a transform such that geometry on the world's origin and axes will move to the cSys origin and axes.` Example: 
`fromWorld(cSys) will (somewhat counterintuitively) perform a transform such that geometry on the cSys origin and axes will move to the world origin and axes.` Example: 
`transform2 * transform1 will perform transform1, followed by transform2.`

symbols.name: opWrap
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, definition is map)
symbols.description:
Wraps or unwraps faces from one surface onto another. The location and orientation of the wrapped faces on the destination surface are controlled by the `anchorPoint` and `anchorDirection` of the `source` and `destination` `WrapSurface`s. The `entities` of the operation are not affected, the result of this operation is a new set of surface bodies or imprinted edges representing the wrapped or unwrapped faces. Faces that are topologically connected will remain topologically connected in the result body for `WrapType.SIMPLE` and `WrapType.TRIM`. This operation currently supports wrapping from a plane onto a cylinder or a cone, and unwrapping from a cylinder or a cone onto a plane.
(Formerly `opRoll`)

subfields.name: wrapType
subfields.description:
The type of wrap to execute. 
 Example: 
`WrapType.SIMPLE wraps entities around the infinite definition of destination.`

subfields.name: entities
subfields.description: Faces to wrap from `source` to `destination`.

subfields.name: source
subfields.description: The surface to wrap from. All `entities` must lie on this surface.

subfields.name: destination
subfields.description: The surface to wrap onto. Must be defined using the `face` field for `WrapType.TRIM` or `WrapType.IMPRINT`.

subfields.name: orientWithDestination
subfields.description: OptionalIf true (default), the normals of the resulting surface will point in the same direction as the `destination`. If false, the normals of the resulting surface will point in the opposite direction. For the purpose of this parameter, the normals of a `WrapSurface` defined by an infinite `Cylinder` are always pointing outwards.

sketch.fs.name: sketch

symbols.name: Sketch
symbols.description:
A `Sketch` object represents a Onshape sketch, to which sketch entities can be added.
Sketches can be created by calls to `newSketch` or `newSketchOnPlane`.

symbols.name: canBeSketch
symbols.description: Typecheck for builtin `Sketch`

symbols.name: isIdForSketch
symbols.description: Check whether an `Id` represents a Sketch operation.

symbols.name: newSketch
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, value is map)
symbols.description:
Create a new sketch on an existing planar entity. The sketch coordinate system follows the canonical plane orientation and the sketch origin is the projection of the world origin onto the plane.
To make a sketch in the coordinate system of an arbitrary `Plane`, use `newSketchOnPlane`.

subfields.name: sketchPlane
subfields.description:
A Query for a single, planar entity. 
 Example: 
`qCreatedBy(makeId("Top"), EntityType.FACE) to sketch on default "Top" plane.`

subfields.name: disableImprinting
subfields.description: OptionalPrevents `sketchPlane` from imprinting on the sketch. Default is `false`.

symbols.name: newSketchOnPlane
symbols.signature: (context is Context, id is Id, value is map)
symbols.description: Create a new sketch on a custom plane, specified by a `Plane` object. The sketch coordinate system will match the coordinate system of the plane.

subfields.name: sketchPlane
subfields.description:
Example: 
`plane(vector(0, 0, 0) * inch, vector(0, 0, 1)) to sketch on the world XY plane.`

symbols.name: skSolve
symbols.description:
Solve any constraints in the sketch and add all sketch entities form the `sketch` to its context.
Even if there are no constraints, a sketch must be solved before its entities are created.

symbols.signature: (sketch is Sketch, textId is string, value is map)
symbols.description: Add a text rectangle to a sketch.

subfields.name: text
subfields.description: A string of text to write. May contain newlines (encoded as `\n`).

subfields.name: fontName
subfields.description:
A font name, with extension ".ttf" or ".otf". To change font weight, replace "-Regular" with "-Bold", "-Italic", or "-BoldItalic".
Must be one of the following fonts:
 Example: 
`"OpenSans-Regular.ttf" Sans-serif font. Default if no match is found.` Example: 
`"AllertaStencil-Regular.ttf" Stencil font. No bold/italic options.` Example: 
`"Arimo-Regular.ttf" Sans-serif font.` Example: 
`"DroidSansMono.ttf" Monospaced sans-serif font. No bold/italic options.` Example: 
`"NotoSans-Regular.ttf" Sans-serif font.` Example: 
`"NotoSansCJKjp-Regular.otf" Japanese font. No italic options.` Example: 
`"NotoSansCJKkr-Regular.otf" Korean font. No italic options.` Example: 
`"NotoSansCJKsc-Regular.otf" Chinese (simplified) font. No italic options.` Example: 
`"NotoSansCJKtc-Regular.otf" Chinese (traditional) font. No italic options.` Example: 
`"NotoSerif-Regular.ttf" Serif font.` Example: 
`"RobotoSlab-Regular.ttf" Sans-serif font. No italic options.` Example: 
`"Tinos-Regular.ttf" Serif font. Metrically compatible with Times New Roman.`

subfields.name: construction
subfields.description: Optional`true` for a construction line

subfields.name: firstCorner

OptionalOne corner of the rectangle into which the text will be placed. Text will start at the left of the rectangle and extend to the right, overflowing the right if necessary. The first line of text will fill the height of the rectangle, with subsequent lines below the rectangle (or above if mirrored vertically). 
 Example: 
`vector(0, 0) * inch`

subfields.name: secondCorner

The first line of text will fill the height of the rectangle, with subsequent lines below the rectangle (or above if mirrored vertically).

query.fs.name: query
query.fs.description: Functions for constructing queries. Features that take queries as inputs should re-export this module.Queries are used to refer to topological entities (vertices, edges, faces, and bodies) that FeatureScript operation and evaluation functions work on. A query is a map that contains instructions for how to find entities. For example, a query for all edges in a context looks like `qEverything(EntityType.EDGE)`. Many queries can take subqueries as arguments, allowing for more complex nested queries.Queries in general do not contain a list of entities in any form. Rather, they contain criteria that specify a subset of the topological entities in a context. To get an array of the entities (if any) which match a query in a context, use `evaluateQuery`. There is no need to evaluate a query before passing it into a function, including any of the Standard Library's operation and evaluation functions.There are two general types of queries: state-based and historical. State-based queries select entities based on the model state, e.g. "All edges adjacent to a cylindrical face which touches this point." Historical queries select entities based on the model history, e.g. "the edge that was generated by feature `extrude_1_id` from sketch vertex `vertex_id` from sketch `sketch_id`." State-based queries cannot refer to entities that have been deleted. Most automatically-generated queries are historical, while queries more commonly used in manually written code are state-based.

symbols.name: Query

A `Query` identifies a specific subset of a context's entities (points, lines, planes, and bodies).
The fields on a Query map depend on its `QueryType`, and may include one or more subqueries.

symbols.name: BodyType
symbols.description:
Specifies the topological type of a body.
All bodies have `EntityType.BODY`, but will generally own many entities of other `EntityType`s.
For example, the result of an extrude with `NewBodyOperationType.NEW` is a body. This body will have `BodyType.SOLID` for a solid extrude, and `BodyType.SHEET` for a surface extrude. This extrude operation will create many geometric entities in the context (faces, edges, or vertices), which are owned by the body, and have the `BodyType` of their owning body.
 See also: `qBodyType`

A query for every part in a part studio is `qBodyType(qEverything(EntityType.BODY), BodyType.SOLID)`
